title: Speaking: Describing and Addressing
learningGoal: Use correct pronouns and verbs in spoken sentences, effectively describing objects using gendered adjectives.
instructions: In this exercise, you will practice navigating formality levels and describing things. You will speak to a shopkeeper (formal) and then to your friend (informal). Pay close attention to your verb endings (hain vs ho).
speakingPrompt: Respond to the prompts using full, grammatically correct Hindi sentences.
scenario: You are at a shop with a friend, talking to the shopkeeper.

subTasks.prompt: You walk into the shop. Greet the elder shopkeeper and ask formally: 'How are you?'
The shopkeeper asks who you are with. Point to your friend and say: 'This is my friend.'
You look at the shop. Tell the shopkeeper: 'The shop is big.' (Shop = Dukan, feminine).
Now turn to your friend. Ask him informally: 'How are you?'

acceptableParaphrases: Using 'Dukan bahut badi hai' (The shop is very big) is acceptable.

criteria.name: Formality Switching
criteria.description: Must successfully use 'Aap...hain' for the shopkeeper and 'Tum...ho' for the friend.

criteria.name: Feminine Adjective
criteria.description: Must use 'badi' instead of 'bada' because Dukan is feminine.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Namaste. Aap kaise hain? Yeh mera dost hai. Dukan badi hai. Tum kaise ho?

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! You are navigating Hindi social registers flawlessly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the 'You' pronouns. Aap takes 'hain'. Tum takes 'ho'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Watch your adjective endings! Dukan is feminine, so it must be 'badi'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: नमस्ते। आप कैसे हैं? यह मेरा दोस्त है। दुकान बड़ी है। तुम कैसे हो?

hintText: Make sure you clearly pronounce the 'n' in 'hain' when talking to the shopkeeper.
commonMistakes: Saying 'Tum kaise hain' or 'Aap kaise ho'.
